name: Render API
what_it_does: Cloud hosting platform API for deploying web services, workers, databases, and static sites with an official MCP server for AI-assisted infrastructure management.
use_cases: Deploying and managing web services via AI agents
not_for: Teams needing Kubernetes or fine-grained container orchestration
best_when: An agent needs to deploy and manage full-stack applications including backends, databases, and workers with minimal DevOps complexity.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-scale infrastructure or fine-grained Kubernetes control.

auth.notes: API key with owner-level or read-only permissions. Key generated from Render account settings. Simple and straightforward for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free web services (spin down after inactivity), free static sites, 90-day PostgreSQL
pricing.notes: Free services spin down after 15min inactivity - acceptable for dev but not production. Very reasonable paid pricing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clean REST API with consistent error formats. Deploy status polling is well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service creation is idempotent by name within a project. Deploy triggers create new deploy IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier services spin down after 15min - cold starts affect agent timeout assumptions
Deploy is async - agents must poll for completion status
Service type (web service vs background worker vs cron) must be correct at creation - cannot change type
MCP server is relatively new (official from Render team)
PostgreSQL databases on free tier are deleted after 90 days

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys per user or service account. No granular scope control — API key has full account access. SOC2 Type II. Render manages TLS certificates automatically. Deploy hooks for CI/CD integration.
